mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds +0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show a statistically significant positive mean during rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicate strong aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL both rise, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat forward returns during recovery periods, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52% in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a collapsing construction sector and reduced consumer confidence, which transmits to broader equity markets and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Housing starts drops of >5% are followed by positive S&P 500 returns in >60% of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating consumer price inflation indicate a restrictive monetary policy response to overheating, which increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising rates and inflation fails to predict negative returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during a mid-cycle expansion phase reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 acceleration yields negative forward returns during mid-cycle periods, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below 50% in a 5-year walk-forward test.
